markdown:
#  Do I need to create a Levelset account for use with Procore Pay? 
#####  General Availability in Select Markets (United States)
![Flag Us](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/eb8f21ce49403a47d7bb5a0cedc7e0e9/7c0ec0977e654cc6bc9f3d8efa80fbe3/flag-us.png?fm=webp&h=14) [Procore Pay ](https://www.procore.com/pay) is available in the United States. It is designed for General Contractors and Owner-Builders who act as their own General Contractors on a job. Procore Pay extends the [Invoice Management](https://www.procore.com/invoice-management) functionality in the Procore web application to handle the payment process between general and specialty contractors.
#####  Procore Pay Integrates with the Modernized Subcontractor Invoicing Experience
in Procore Pay, lien wavers are powered by Levelset, a Procore Company. To manage lien waivers with the Invoice Management tools in Procore, your Procore users must be using the modernized subcontractor invoicing experience.
##  Background 
Lien waivers for Procore Pay are powered by [Levelset](https://www.levelset.com/) , a Procore Company.
##  Answer 
No. Procore Pay customers do NOT create their own Levelset accounts. Instead, the Procore Pay software:
  * Creates a company account in Levelset when a Payments Admin turns the Lien Waiver Settings ON in the Company level Payments tool. If your company already has a Procore Pay account, be sure to tell your Procore point of contact during the Procore Pay implementation process.
  * Stores the required account information to provide users with the ability to seamlessly access your lien waiver templates in the Procore Pay software and the Invoice Management tools in the Procore web application.


#####  Note
If you have an existing Levelset account, another user without one must enable **Lien Waivers for Procore Pay**. A pre-existing Levelset account can block you from activating this feature yourself. If your company already has a Levelset account, be sure to tell your Procore point of contact during the Procore Pay implementation process. 
#####  Tip
**Managing lien waivers in Procore Pay?** Your company's Payments Admin manages the lien waiver settings and templates in the Company level Payments tool. On Procore projects, an invoice administrator enables lien waiver templates and sets the default templates. To learn more, see: 
  * [Enable Lien Waivers in the Company Payments Tool](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/procore-pay/tutorials/enable-lien-waivers-and-set-default-templates-on-projects)
  * [Enable Lien Waivers & Set Default Templates on Projects](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/procore-pay/tutorials/enable-lien-waivers-and-set-default-templates-on-projects)


## See Also
  * [What variables can we include as placeholders in a lien waiver template?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-what-variables-can-we-include-as-placeholders-in-a-lien-waiver-template)
  * [When does Procore Pay send email notifications?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-when-does-procore-pay-send-email-notifications)
  * [What are joint payments and joint checks?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-what-are-joint-payments-and-joint-checks)
title: Do I need to create a Levelset account for use with Procore Pay?
description: Procore Pay users do not need to create their own Levelset accounts when using lien waivers with Procore Pay.
